questions.q: What were the main features of the Permanent Settlement of 1793?
questions.answer: The main features of the Permanent Settlement of 1793 were the fixation of revenue, creation of a new class of landholders, and the introduction of a new system of land administration.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______ Act of 1833 abolished the monopoly of the East India Company over Indian trade.
Describe the impact of the British land revenue policy on the Indian peasantry during the colonial period.
questions.answer: The British land revenue policy led to the exploitation of the Indian peasantry, as they were forced to pay high revenues, leading to poverty, indebtedness, and the loss of land. The policy also led to the creation of a new class of landholders, who were loyal to the British.

questions.q: What were the consequences of the commercialization of agriculture in India during the colonial period?
questions.answer: The commercialization of agriculture led to the growth of cash crops, the expansion of trade, and the increase in agricultural production. However, it also led to the exploitation of the peasantry, the displacement of traditional crops, and the increase in rural poverty.

questions.q: True or False: The British introduced the ryotwari system in all parts of India.
What were the main differences between the Permanent Settlement and the ryotwari system?
questions.answer: The main differences between the Permanent Settlement and the ryotwari system were the fixation of revenue, the creation of a new class of landholders, and the introduction of a new system of land administration. The Permanent Settlement was introduced in Bengal, while the ryotwari system was introduced in Madras.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______ peasant movement was a major challenge to the British colonial rule in India.
Describe the role of the East India Company in the exploitation of Indian resources during the colonial period.
questions.answer: The East India Company played a significant role in the exploitation of Indian resources, as it was granted a monopoly over Indian trade and was allowed to collect revenue on behalf of the British government. The company exploited Indian resources, including textiles, indigo, and tea, and used its power to oppress the Indian peasantry.

questions.q: What were the consequences of the introduction of new agricultural technologies in India during the colonial period?
questions.answer: The introduction of new agricultural technologies led to the increase in agricultural production, the expansion of trade, and the growth of cash crops. However, it also led to the displacement of traditional crops, the increase in rural poverty, and the exploitation of the peasantry.

questions.q: MCQ: Which of the following was a consequence of the British land revenue policy in India? (A) Increase in agricultural production, (B) Decrease in rural poverty, (C) Exploitation of the peasantry, (D) Growth of industry
